<<Economic Stress and Substance Abuse Analysis>>
To find the peak, we locate the vertex of the parabola at an economic stress index of 2, which yields a maximum of 13 incidents.
This human behavior pattern is as unpredictable as a baseball game, yet the math remains clear.
To keep incidents below 20, the community must maintain an economic stress index between 0 and approximately 3.62.
Just as water flows around obstacles, policy must adapt to these economic constraints in the western region.
Any stress level outside this safe range risks exceeding the desired limit for substance abuse.
Therefore, keeping stress low is crucial for community health.
